name: DEPLOYMENT
short_description: An intense competitive top-down shooter: choose your class, capture turrets, progress, and dominate in the arena. top-down shooter, multiplayer shooter, arena shooter, class-based shooter, sci-fi shooter
detailed_description:
Deployment is a dynamic competitive top-down shooter where infosoldiers (units of pure data) battle for control over the digital systems that run the world. Deploy troops from five unique classes, fight on randomly generated maps, capture system turrets, and defeat your enemies.

Story / Setting
After the Last War, civilization is on the brink of collapse — but computers still manage the key systems (energy, transport, factories). Whoever controls these systems controls the world. In this digital war, governments and megacorporations send infosoldiers into battle to seize the Etherweb.

Gameplay

  • Choose one of five classes: Gunner, Pyro, Sniper, Cyclops, Rocketeer — each with unique skills and playstyle.

  • Engage in intense 5v5 or free-for-all battles on randomly generated arenas.

  • Capture and defend system turrets to gain tactical advantages.

  • Earn experience, upgrade your class, and unlock new abilities.

  • Play in multiplayer or train solo against AI bots.

  • Climb the leaderboards, cooperate with your squad, and develop your own playstyle.

Key Features
• Fast-paced competitive top-down shooter
• Randomly generated maps — every battle is unique
• Five distinct classes with different weapons and playstyles
• System turret capture as a key tactical mechanic
• Single-player mode with bots for practice
• Leaderboards, progression, and class customization
• Sci-fi style and abstract design focused on gameplay

Keywords (EN)
top-down shooter, arena shooter, competitive game, multiplayer shooter, fast-paced shooter, reflex shooter, team-based shooter, sci-fi shooter, cyberpunk shooter, class-based shooter, arcade shooter, online battles, 5v5 arena, free-for-all, turret capture, leaderboards, character progression, random maps, infosoldiers, Etherweb, network arcade
